title: OpenClaw video generation does not use openai-codex OAuth in the installed runtime
page: Lobster Stage 4 video generation through OpenClaw video_generate
symptom: Aries/Lobster delegates video generation to OpenClaw's video_generate tool when LOBSTER_MEDIA_GATEWAY_ENABLED=1 and LOBSTER_VIDEO_RENDER_ENABLED=1, but the installed OpenClaw 2026.4.26 OpenAI video provider only checks provider="openai" and throws "OpenAI API key missing" when no direct OpenAI provider key exists. Unlike image generation, it has no openai-codex OAuth fallback path.
acceptance_criteria: OpenClaw video provider exposes a documented openai-codex OAuth path for OpenAI video models, or Aries documentation clearly states that Codex OAuth is image-only in the current OpenClaw runtime.
A live or mocked video_generate smoke proves the selected auth route without printing credential values.
Aries/Lobster continues to delegate video requests through OpenClaw and does not reimplement provider OAuth internally.
notes: Deferred as an OpenClaw provider/runtime gap, not an Aries app source fix. Aries should keep delegating video_generate to OpenClaw.
